Center: Russian Nuclear Structure and Reaction Data Centre, Kurchatov Institute, Moscow, Russia
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.53, p.2486 (1996)
title: Determination of the 9Be(alpha,n)12C reaction rate

x4freetext: Determination of the 9Be(alpha,n)12C reaction rate

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.53, p.2486 (1996)

x4freetext: The beryllium targets were solid metallic targets
evaporated on a copper backing. The exact determination
of the target thickness has been done using the narrow
resonance at alpha-particle energy=620 keV.
The detector consists of a cylindrical
polyethylene moderator and a matrix of 16 3He-filled
proportional counters.
The beam energy has been calibrated using
graphite targets and a narrow resonance at 1056.3-keV
of the reaction 13C(alpha,n)16O.

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.53, p.2486 (1996)

x4freetext: Tables were
received from R.Kunz. (Data presented on figures in
the article.)

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: No information about source of uncertainty.
Analytical expression of the reaction rate is
given.
Astrophysical factor.

x4code.actHelp: Data received at the data center

x4freetext: The data tables were received from R.Kunz.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR. Subents 006 and 007 were merged.
Yield curve. No any other information about units.
- By authors: Because the 9Be(alpha,n)12C yield is
several orders of magnitude larger than the yield from
the backing, neutrons arising from background reactions
could be neglected.

x4freetext: - By Compiler:
1. With the 4-pi detector a rough energy determination
of the neutron energies could be performed making use
of the energy dependence of neutron diffusion.
2. Van der Zwan and Geiger (see REL-REF) showed that
the yield contribution of the second excited state
of 12C group is rather small for energies < 3.7 MeV,
therefore the group was not considered in this
investigation.
